topic: Perplexity AI search methodology, answer synthesis patterns

entries.a: Vane is a privacy-focused AI answering engine that runs entirely on your own hardware.

entries.q: What are the search modes available in Vane?
entries.a: Vane offers three search modes: Speed Mode, Balanced Mode, and Quality Mode.
Vane can search the web, discussions, academic papers, and more sources are being added.
Vane ensures privacy by running entirely on your own hardware and using SearxNG for web search, which keeps your identity private.

entries.q: Can Vane be installed without Docker?
entries.a: Yes, Vane can be installed without Docker by cloning the repository, installing dependencies, building the application, and starting it.

entries.q: What is SearxNG and how is it used in Vane?
entries.a: SearxNG is a search engine that Vane uses for web search. It is included in the Docker image and can also be used with a separate instance.

entries.q: What AI providers does Vane support?
entries.a: Vane supports local LLMs through Ollama and cloud providers like OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Groq, and more.

entries.q: How can I get started with Vane using Docker?
entries.a: To get started with Vane using Docker, run the command 'docker run -d -p 3000:3000 -v vane-data:/home/vane/data --name vane itzcrazykns1337/vane:latest' and access Vane at http://localhost:3000.
